sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Club Brugge KV has shown strong form in their last 5 matches, winning 3, drawing 1, and losing 1, with a dominant 6-0 victory showcasing their attacking prowess. KVC Westerlo has had mixed results, winning 3 and losing 2 in their last 5, with inconsistency in defense evident from conceding 4 goals in one match. Brugge's superior league position (4th) compared to Westerlo (13th) further supports their edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Club Brugge KV has won both games against KVC Westerlo with scores of 4-3 and 2-1, indicating their historical dominance. Both matches saw goals from both sides, suggesting Westerlo can score but struggles to contain Brugge's attack.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Club Brugge KV excels with high ball possession (62%), top rankings in corners per game (6.7), and a solid defensive record (least goals conceded rank: 2). Westerlo, while capable of scoring (11 goals this season), has defensive vulnerabilities (13 goals conceded) and lacks consistency in creating big chances compared to Brugge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Hans Vanaken has been pivotal for Brugge with an average Sofascore rating of 8.34 and 3 goals in the last 5 matches, posing a significant threat. Westerlo lacks standout in-form players in recent data, which could limit their ability to challenge Brugge’s defense. Trends also show Brugge often scoring first in head-to-heads and matches.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, KVC Westerlo leads Club Brugge KV by a surprising 1-0 scoreline at Jan Breydel Stadium. Despite Club Brugge dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third and penalty area, they have been unable to convert their chances into goals. Westerlo, on the other hand, capitalized early with a goal in the first minute and added two more right at the end of the half, showcasing clinical finishing. The home side's frustration is evident as they missed a big chance and struggled to break through Westerlo's defensive setup.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Club Brugge's attacking efforts
halftimeSections.text: Club Brugge has shown significant intent in attack, pushing forward with numerous entries into dangerous areas. Their dominance in possession is clear, and they have created several opportunities, including a notable big chance. Players like Hans Vanaken and Christos Tzolis have been central to their attacking plays, with Vanaken creating key opportunities. However, their inability to finish has left them trailing despite their control of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Westerlo's defensive resilience and counter-attacks
halftimeSections.text: KVC Westerlo has adopted a more pragmatic approach, focusing on defending deep and striking on the counter. Their early goal gave them confidence, and their late flurry of goals just before the break has put them in a commanding position. While they’ve had less of the ball and fewer entries into the final third, their efficiency in front of goal, with contributions from players like Josimar Alcócer and Nacho Ferri, has been the difference. Defensively, they’ve held firm against Club Brugge’s pressure, with key clearances and interceptions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half started with a sharp attacking spell from Westerlo, catching Club Brugge off guard right at the outset. After the early setback, Club Brugge quickly shifted the momentum in their favor, maintaining consistent pressure through much of the half with sustained attacking phases. However, Westerlo regained some momentum in brief spells, particularly towards the latter part of the half, leading to their additional goals. Club Brugge needs to capitalize on their attacking dominance in the second half to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Club Brugge, Hans Vanaken stands out as a pivotal figure with his ability to create chances and drive the team forward. Christos Tzolis also remains a threat upfront despite missing a big chance. On the Westerlo side, Josimar Alcócer has been instrumental with his goal and assist, while Nacho Ferri’s finishing could prove decisive if they get more opportunities. Both teams have players capable of changing the game in the second half.
